verse_text: Be sober, be vigilant; because your adversary the devil walks about like a roaring lion, seeking whom he may devour.

links.context: The historical background of Ps 7 is Absalom's rebellion against his father, King David. This history is found in 2Kg 14-18. King David and his army prefigure Christ and the Church, whereas Absalom and his army represent the devil and his angels. Thus, Ps 7 teaches the Church how to pray in this warfare: (1) The faithful place their hope in God for deliverance from the enemy, that is the devil, called a lion (v. 3; see also [[1PE.5:8]]). (2) Personal virtue in the Church is essential for successful warfare against this enemy (vv. 4-17). (3) The credit for any victory achieved goes to the Lord (v. 18).
